description: Canonical rDNA-style: 4 × 10 kb host units, each with a 3 kb sub-repeat block (200 bp × 15) in the middle.
8 × 2 kb host units, sub-repeat 500 bp (50 bp × 10).
4 × 30 kb host units, sub-repeat 5 kb (500 bp × 10).
Same 10 kb host scale as rdna_like, but only 2 of 4 host units (#1 and #3) carry the sub-repeat; the other 2 are host-only with a random filler of equal length.
Same 10 kb host scale, but the sub-repeat sits near the start of each host unit (host_A short, host_B long).
10 kb host, but the embedded sub-repeat is short (only 1 kb = 200 bp × 5). Tests whether tiny blocks still light up.
Negative control: pure 200 bp tandem repeat, 200 copies (40 kb). No host structure, no sub-repeat blocks. The scanner should report NO sub-repeat flag.
Negative control: clean HOR k=5 (founder 200 bp, tile 1000 bp), 40 copies of the tile (40 kb). Uniform hierarchical structure. The scanner should report NO sub-repeat flag.

Pure (CA) dinucleotide tandem repeat, 2000 copies (4 kb). SSR scanner should flag yes with dominant motif AC (CA normalises to AC), ~100%% coverage. rule_proto + subrepeat should report unresolved/none (period 2 sits below their min_period floor).

Tandem repeat of a 200-bp monomer that contains a (GA)50 = 100 bp SSR patch in its middle. 100 monomer copies = 20 kb. rule_proto should call simple_TR (founder 200), subrepeat should NOT fire (no spatial alternation — the SSR is everywhere by design), SSR should flag yes (dominant motif AG, ~50%% coverage).
